audit_status.source_base: Greek Hebrews text tradition with priesthood and Psalm citation texture; draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for high priest taken from humans, Melchizedek citation, learned obedience, milk/solid food warning.

verses.restored: For every high priest taken from humans is appointed on behalf of humans in things toward the Divine, so that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices concerning sins/misalignments,
being able to deal gently with the ignorant and wandering, since he himself is also surrounded with weakness.
And because of this he owes, just as concerning the people, so also concerning himself, to offer for sins/misalignments.
And no one takes the honor to oneself, but being called by the Divine, just as Aaron also.
So also Messiah did not glorify himself to become high priest, but the One speaking to him: You are my Son; today I have begotten you.
Just as also in another place he says: You are priest into the age according to the order of Melchizedek.
Who in the days of his flesh, with loud cries and tears, having offered petitions and supplications to the One able to save/heal him out of death, and being heard because of reverent devotion,
though being Son, learned obedience from what he suffered,
and having been completed, became cause/source of age-long salvation/healing to all those obeying him,
being addressed by the Divine as high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
Concerning whom the word for us is much and hard to explain, since you have become sluggish in hearing.

For indeed, though owing to be teachers by reason of time, again you have need for someone to teach you the beginning elements of the oracles of the Divine, and you have become having need of milk, not solid food.
For everyone sharing milk is inexperienced in word of righteousness/justice, for one is infant.
But solid food is for mature ones, those having faculties trained through practice for discernment of both beautiful/good and harmful.

translation_notes: High priesthood begins in gentle solidarity with weakness, not status elevation.
Melchizedek language should be handled as Psalm/canonical argument, not speculative overreach.
Jesus learning obedience through suffering must not romanticize harm; it names faithful embodiment under pressure.
safety_note: The milk/solid-food warning is communal exhortation toward discernment, not contempt for beginners.
